children.label: I
children.text: was fully operational as a live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator, a relevant museum operator, a motion picture theatre operator, or a talent representative on ; andFebruary 29, 2020

children.label: II
children.text: has gross earned revenue during the first, second, third, or, only with respect to an application submitted on or after , fourth quarter in 2020 that demonstrates not less than a 25 percent reduction from the gross earned revenue of the live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator, the relevant museum operator, the motion picture theatre operator, or the talent representative during the same quarter in 2019.January 1, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: the live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator is or intends to resume organizing, promoting, producing, managing, or hosting future live events described in paragraph (3)(A)(i);

children.label: II
children.text: the motion picture theatre operator is open or intends to reopen for the primary purpose of public exhibition of motion pictures;

children.label: III
children.text: the relevant museum operator is open or intends to reopen; or

children.label: IV
children.text: the talent representative is representing or managing artists and entertainers.

children.label: I
children.text: A defined performance and audience space.

children.label: II
children.text: Mixing equipment, a public address system, and a lighting rig.

children.label: IV
children.text: There is a paid ticket or cover charge to attend most performances and artists are paid fairly and do not play for free or solely for tips, except for fundraisers or similar charitable events.

children.label: V
children.text: For a venue owned or operated by a nonprofit entity that produces free events, the events are produced and managed primarily by paid employees, not by volunteers.

children.label: VI
children.text: Performances are marketed through listings in printed or electronic publications, on websites, by mass email, or on social media.

children.label: I
children.text: At least 1 auditorium that includes a motion picture screen and fixed audience seating.

children.label: II
children.text: A projection booth or space containing not less than 1 motion picture projector.

children.label: III
children.text: A paid ticket charge to attend exhibition of motion pictures.

children.label: IV
children.text: Motion picture exhibitions are marketed through showtime listings in printed or electronic publications, on websites, by mass mail, or on social media.

children.label: I
children.text: Serving as a relevant museum as its principal business activity.

children.label: II
children.text: Indoor exhibition spaces that are a component of the principal business activity and which have been subjected to pandemic-related occupancy restrictions.

children.label: III
children.text: At least 1 auditorium, theater, or performance or lecture hall with fixed audience seating and regular programming.

children.label: aa
children.text: Being an issuer, the securities of which are listed on a national securities exchange.

children.label: bb
children.text: Receiving more than 10 percent of gross revenue from Federal funding during 2019, excluding amounts received by the live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator, the relevant museum operator, the motion picture theatre operator, or the talent representative under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5121

children.label: aa
children.text: Owning or operating venues, relevant museums, motion picture theatres, or talent agencies or talent management companies in more than 1 country.

children.label: bb
children.text: Owning or operating venues, relevant museums, motion picture theatres, or talent agencies or talent management companies in more than 10 States.

children.label: cc
children.text: Employing more than 500 employees as of , determined on a full-time equivalent basis in accordance with subparagraph (C).February 29, 2020

children.label: aa
children.text: shall be the live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator, the relevant museum operator, the motion picture theatre operator, or the talent representative; and

children.label: bb
children.text: shall not include entities of the State or political subdivision other than the live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator, the relevant museum operator, the motion picture theatre operator, or the talent representative.

children.label: i
children.text: presents live performances of a prurient sexual nature; or

children.label: ii
children.text: derives, directly or indirectly, more than de minimis gross revenue through the sale of products or services, or the presentation of any depictions or displays, of a prurient sexual nature.

children.label: i
children.text: any employee working not fewer than 30 hours per week shall be considered a full-time employee; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any employee working not fewer than 10 hours and fewer than 30 hours per week shall be counted as one-half of a full-time employee.

children.heading: Multiple business entities
children.label: D Multiple business entities
children.text: Each business entity of an eligible person or entity that also meets the requirements under subparagraph (A) and that is not described in subparagraph (B) shall be treated by the Administrator as an independent, non-affiliated entity for the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Exchange; issuer; security
children.label: 2 Exchange; issuer; security
children.text: The terms “exchange”, “issuer”, and “security” have the meanings given those terms in .section 78c(a) of this title

children.label: 3 Live venue operator or promoter, theatrical producer, or live performing arts organization operator
aa

children.label: bb

children.label: II
children.text: for which not less than 70 percent of the earned revenue of the individual or entity is generated through, to the extent related to a live event described in subclause (I), cover charges or ticket sales, production fees or production reimbursements, nonprofit educational initiatives, or the sale of event beverages, food, or merchandise; or

children.label: I

children.heading: National securities exchange
children.label: 5 National securities exchange
children.text: The term “national securities exchange” means an exchange registered as a national securities exchange under .section 78f of this title

children.heading: Nonprofit
children.label: 6 Nonprofit
children.text: The term “nonprofit”, with respect to an organization, means that the organization is exempt from taxation under .section 501(a) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: has the meaning given the term “museum” in ; andsection 9172 of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: shall not include any entity that is organized as a for-profit entity.

children.heading: Seasonal employer
children.label: 8 Seasonal employer
children.text: The term “seasonal employer” has the meaning given that term in subparagraph (A) of , as amended by this Act.section 636(a)(36) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a State;

children.label: B
children.text: the District of Columbia;

children.label: C
children.text: the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: as not less than 70 percent of the operations of the agent or manager, is engaged in representing or managing artists and entertainers;

children.label: ii
children.text: books or represents musicians, comedians, actors, or similar performing artists primarily at live events in venues or at festivals; and

children.label: iii
children.text: represents performers described in clause (ii) that are paid in an amount that is based on the number of tickets sold, or a similar basis; and

children.label: iv

children.heading: Administration
children.label: A Administration
children.text: The Associate Administrator for the Office of Disaster Assistance of the Administration shall coordinate and formulate policies relating to the administration of grants made under this section.

children.heading: Certification of need
children.label: B Certification of need
children.text: An eligible person or entity applying for a grant under this section shall submit a good faith certification that the uncertainty of current economic conditions makes necessary the grant to support the ongoing operations of the eligible person or entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator may make initial grants to eligible persons or entities in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Initial priorities for awarding grants
children.label: B Initial priorities for awarding grants

children.heading: First priority in awarding grants
children.label: i First priority in awarding grants
children.text: During the initial 14-day period during which the Administrator awards grants under this paragraph, the Administrator shall only award grants to an eligible person or entity with revenue, during the period beginning on and ending on , that is not more than 10 percent of the revenue of the eligible person or entity during the period beginning on and ending on , due to the COVID–19 pandemic.April 1, 2020December 31, 2020April 1, 2019December 31, 2019

children.heading: Second priority in awarding grants
children.label: ii Second priority in awarding grants
children.text: During the 14-day period immediately following the 14-day period described in clause (i), the Administrator shall only award grants to an eligible person or entity with revenue, during the period beginning on and ending on , that is not more than 30 percent of the revenue of the eligible person or entity during the period beginning on and ending on , due to the COVID–19 pandemic.April 1, 2020December 31, 2020April 1, 2019December 31, 2019

children.label: I
children.text: any amounts received by an eligible person or entity under the CARES Act (; ) or an amendment made by the CARES Act shall not be counted as revenue of an eligible person or entity;Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 281

children.label: II
children.text: the Administrator shall use an accrual method of accounting for determining revenue; and

children.label: III
children.text: the Administrator may use alternative methods to establish revenue losses for an eligible person or entity that is a seasonal employer and that would be adversely impacted if January, February, and March are excluded from the calculation of year-over-year revenues.

children.heading: Limit on use of amounts for priority applicants
children.label: iv Limit on use of amounts for priority applicants
children.text: The Administrator may use not more than 80 percent of the amounts appropriated under section 323(d)(1)(H) of this Act to carry out this section to make initial grants under this paragraph to eligible persons or entities described in clause (i) or (ii) of this subparagraph that apply for a grant under this paragraph during the initial 28-day period during which the Administrator awards grants under this paragraph.

children.heading: Grants after priority periods
children.label: C Grants after priority periods
children.text: After the end of the initial 28-day period during which the Administrator awards grants under this paragraph, the Administrator may award an initial grant to any eligible person or entity.

children.heading: Limits on number of initial grants to affiliates
children.label: D Limits on number of initial grants to affiliates
children.text: Not more than 5 business entities of an eligible person or entity that would be considered affiliates under the affiliation rules of the Administration may receive a grant under this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), not less than $2,000,000,000 of the total amount of grants made available under this paragraph shall be awarded to eligible persons or entities which employ not more than 50 full-time employees, determined in accordance with subsection (a)(1)(C).

children.heading: Time limit
children.label: ii Time limit
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply on and after the date that is 60 days after the Administrator begins awarding grants under this section and, on and after such date, amounts available for grants under this section may be used for grants under this section to any eligible person or entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Administrator may make a supplemental grant in accordance with this section to an eligible person or entity that receives a grant under paragraph (2) if, as of , the revenues of the eligible person or entity for the most recent calendar quarter are not more than 30 percent of the revenues of the eligible person or entity for the corresponding calendar quarter during 2019 due to the COVID–19 pandemic.April 1, 2021

children.heading: Processing timely initial grant applications first
children.label: B Processing timely initial grant applications first
children.text: The Administrator may not award a supplemental grant under subparagraph (A) until the Administrator has completed processing (including determining whether to award a grant) each application for an initial grant under paragraph (2) that is submitted by an eligible person or entity on or before the date that is 60 days after the date on which the Administrator begins accepting such applications.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: 4 Certification
children.text: An eligible person or entity applying for a grant under this section that is an eligible business described in the matter preceding subclause (I) of section 4003(c)(3)(D)(i) of the CARES Act (), shall make a good-faith certification described in subclauses (IX) and (X) of such section.15 U.S.C. 9042(c)(3)(D)(i)

children.label: I
children.text: for an eligible person or entity that was in operation on , the amount equal to 45 percent of the gross earned revenue of the eligible person or entity during 2019; orJanuary 1, 2019

children.label: aa
children.text: the average monthly gross earned revenue for each full month during which the eligible person or entity was in operation during 2019; by

children.label: bb
children.text: 6; or

children.heading: Application to relevant museum operators
children.label: B Application to relevant museum operators
children.text: A relevant museum operator may not receive grants under subsection (b)(2) in a total amount that is more than $10,000,000 with respect to all relevant museums operated by the relevant museum operator.

children.heading: Reduction for recipients of new PPP loans
children.label: C Reduction for recipients of new PPP loans

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The otherwise applicable amount of a grant under subsection (b)(2) to an eligible person or entity shall be reduced by the total amount of loans guaranteed under paragraph (36) or (37) of that are received on or after by the eligible person or entity.section 636(a) of this titleDecember 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: shall be the eligible person or entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall not include entities of the State or political subdivision other than the eligible person or entity.

children.heading: Supplemental grants
children.label: 2 Supplemental grants
children.text: A grant under subsection (b)(3) shall be in the amount equal to 50 percent of the grant received by the eligible person or entity under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Overall maximums
children.label: 3 Overall maximums
children.text: The total amount of grants received under paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (b) by an eligible person or entity shall be not more than $10,000,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), amounts received under a grant under this section may be used for costs incurred during the period beginning on , and ending on .March 1, 2020December 31, 2021

children.heading: Extension for supplemental grants
children.label: ii Extension for supplemental grants
children.text: If an eligible person or entity receives a grant under subsection (b)(3), amounts received under either grant under this section may be used for costs incurred during the period beginning on , and ending on .March 1, 2020June 30, 2022

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), an eligible person or entity shall return to the Administrator any amounts received under a grant under this section that are not expended on or before the date that is 1 year after the date of disbursement of the grant.

children.heading: Extension for supplemental grants
children.label: ii Extension for supplemental grants
children.text: If an eligible person or entity receives a grant under subsection (b)(3), the eligible person or entity shall return to the Administrator any amounts received under either grant under this section that are not expended on or before the date that is 18 months after the date of disbursement to the eligible person or entity of the grant under subsection (b)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: the terms “covered mortgage obligation”, “covered rent obligation”, “covered utility payment”, and “covered worker protection expenditure” have the meanings given those terms in , as redesignated, transferred, and amended by this Act; andsection 636m(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the term “payroll costs” has the meaning given that term in .section 636(a)(36)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: payroll costs;

children.label: ii
children.text: payments on any covered rent obligation;

children.label: iii
children.text: any covered utility payment;

children.label: iv
children.text: scheduled payments of interest or principal on any covered mortgage obligation (which shall not include any prepayment of principal on a covered mortgage obligation);

children.label: v
children.text: scheduled payments of interest or principal on any indebtedness or debt instrument (which shall not include any prepayment of principal) incurred in the ordinary course of business that is a liability of the eligible person or entity and was incurred prior to ;February 15, 2020

children.label: vi
children.text: covered worker protection expenditures;

children.label: vii
children.text: payments made to independent contractors, as reported on Form–1099 MISC, not to exceed a total of $100,000 in annual compensation for any individual employee of an independent contractor; and

children.label: I
children.text: maintenance expenses;

children.label: II
children.text: administrative costs, including fees and licensing costs;

children.label: III
children.text: State and local taxes and fees;

children.label: IV
children.text: operating leases in effect as of ;February 15, 2020

children.label: V
children.text: payments required for insurance on any insurance policy; and

children.label: VI
children.text: advertising, production transportation, and capital expenditures related to producing a theatrical or live performing arts production, concert, exhibition, or comedy show, except that a grant under this section may not be used primarily for such expenditures.

children.label: A
children.text: to purchase real estate;

children.label: B
children.text: for payments of interest or principal on loans originated after ;February 15, 2020

children.label: C
children.text: to invest or re-lend funds;

children.label: D
children.text: for contributions or expenditures to, or on behalf of, any political party, party committee, or candidate for elective office; or

children.label: E
children.text: for any other use as may be prohibited by the Administrator.

children.heading: Increased oversight of shuttered venue operator grants
children.label: e Increased oversight of shuttered venue operator grants
A
children.text: with respect to employment records, for the 4-year period following receipt of the grant; and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to other records, for the 3-year period following receipt of the grant.

children.label: A
children.text: review and audit grants under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: require repayment of misspent funds; or

children.label: ii
children.text: pursue legal action to collect funds.

children.heading: Shuttered venue oversight and audit plan
children.label: f Shuttered venue oversight and audit plan
A
children.text: the policies and procedures of the Administrator for conducting oversight and audits of grants under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the metrics that the Administrator shall use to determine which grants under this section will be audited pursuant to subsection (e).

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of initial grants approved and disbursed;

children.label: B
children.text: the total amount of grants received by each eligible person or entity, including any supplemental grants;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of active investigations and audits of grants under this section;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of completed reviews and audits of grants under this section, including a description of any findings of fraud or other material noncompliance.1So in original. The period probably should be “; and”.1

children.label: E
children.text: any substantial changes made to the oversight and audit plan submitted under paragraph (1).
